title: Language of Birds
paragraphs: Language of Birds explores the virtues of translation and the desire for communication between humans and birds. Narrated by a storyteller from the future, after the sixth extinction, the film observes attempts to establish a possible exchange.
Field surveys were conducted between March and October 2024. Each session combines a direct-contact recording (piezoelectric microphone) with an ink botanical drawing protocol.

title: Manières d'être vivant
caption: Manières d'être vivant, Baptiste Morizot, La Terre au carré, 2020

Field surveys were conducted between March and October 2024. Each session combines a direct-contact recording (piezoelectric microphone) with an ink botanical drawing protocol.

blockquote.text: « Le bruissement, c'est le bruit de ce qui fonctionne bien. »
blockquote.cite: — Roland Barthes, Le bruissement de la langue, 1984

title: Latency and Sound Perception

image.caption: Presentation at the Arts and Cognition seminar, Paris, June 2024. 18'.

paragraphs: This series of experiments explores the perceptual boundary between actual and anticipated sound. In collaboration with Laboratory X, 40 participants were subjected to biased listening protocols.<sup class="cit">[3]</sup>
Results suggest the body anticipates the sound source approximately 80 ms before conscious perception, consistent with Noë's work on embodied perception.<sup class="cit">[4]</sup>

refs.text: Zatorre, R. J. & Salimpoor, V. N. (2013). From perception to pleasure. PNAS, 110(2), 10430–10437.
Noë, A. (2004). Action in Perception. MIT Press.

title: Chromogenic Sequences
paragraphs: A series of twenty photographs exploring the relationship between light spectrum and emotional state. Exhibition presented at Gallery Y, Paris, March–April 2024.
